questions: According to the official Mac User Guide and support documentation, distinct procedures are recommended for different types of security and maintenance scenarios. Identify the specific remedial action cited for a user who has 'forgotten' the password to their primary Apple account versus the action cited for a user who has explicitly 'deleted' a password or passkey. Additionally, name the two system utilities described: one designed to reinstall the operating system without removing personal data, and one designed to automatically back up data to an external storage device.

Identify the official website that hosts the 'Mac User Guide' and provides support for the ecosystem featuring 'Apple Intelligence'. Locate the 'Terms of Use' agreement governing this site. Based on the text of this agreement, extract the following specific legal details: (1) The exact maximum monetary liability (in US Dollars) the site owner assumes for damages that are not related to subscription fees; (2) The specific US County and State designated as the exclusive venue for dispute resolution; and (3) The list of four specific automated devices or methodologies (starting with 'deep-link') that are explicitly prohibited for accessing or monitoring the site's content.

rounds.question: According to the official Mac User Guide and support documentation, distinct procedures are recommended for different types of security and maintenance scenarios. Identify the specific remedial action cited for a user who has 'forgotten' the password to their primary Apple account versus the action cited for a user who has explicitly 'deleted' a password or passkey. Additionally, name the two system utilities described: one designed to reinstall the operating system without removing personal data, and one designed to automatically back up data to an external storage device.
checklist_width: Scenario 1 (Primary Account): Identify the action as resetting the Apple ID password.
Scenario 2 (Deleted Credential): Identify the action as recovering a deleted password or passkey.
Scenario 3 (OS Reinstall): Identify the tool as 'macOS Recovery'.
Scenario 4 (Backup): Identify the tool as 'Time Machine' and note the requirement of an external storage device.
checklist_depth: Distinction Validation: The answer must clearly distinguish between the broad account recovery (Apple ID) found in the Support section and the specific item recovery (Deleted Password) found in the Getting Started/User Guide section.
System Tool Identification: Must correctly map the 'reinstall without data loss' description to 'macOS Recovery' and 'automatic backup' to 'Time Machine'.
rounds.rationale: This question requires 'Deep' reasoning to distinguish between two semantically similar but technically distinct problems (forgetting an account password vs. deleting a saved credential) which are treated differently in the source text. It is 'Wide' because the answer requires aggregating information from two different sections of the knowledge base: the 'Getting Started' section (Source A) which mentions recovering deleted passwords, and the 'macOS Support' section (Source B) which covers Apple ID, macOS Recovery, and Time Machine.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: macOS Support Reset your Apple ID password If you forgot your Apple ID password, you can reset it and regain access to your account. Learn how to reset your password Reinstall
rounds.answer_a:
For a user who has **forgotten** their primary Apple account (Apple ID) password, official support documentation recommends resetting it via the iForgot website (iforgot.apple.com), onscreen instructions in System Settings > [your name] > Sign-In & Security > Change Password on a trusted device, or account recovery processes including a recovery key or recovery contact if standard reset fails.[4][6][7][8] This contrasts with a user who has **explicitly deleted** a password or passkey, where Apple guides direct using the recovery key (set up via System Settings > [your name] > Sign-In & Security > Recovery Key) alongside a verification code to regain access, as deletion often triggers enhanced security requiring this specific tool rather than standard password reset flows.[3]

The two system utilities are **macOS Reinstall** (available in macOS Recovery mode by holding the power button or Command-R on Intel Macs, selecting Reinstall macOS), which reinstalls the operating system while preserving personal data, apps, and settings; and **Time Machine**, which automatically backs up data to an external storage device (e.g., drive connected via USB) via System Settings > General > Time Machine, enabling full system restores without data loss.[2][5]

These procedures ensure security distinctions: forgotten passwords leverage account-linked recovery, while deleted credentials prioritize recovery keys to prevent unauthorized access. Always boot into Recovery for login-related issues if login window options fail.[1][2]

(Word count: 248)

## References
[1] https://blog.lastpass.com/posts/forgot-apple-id-password  
[2] https://support.apple.com/en-ca/102633  
[3] https://support.apple.com/en-us/109345  
[4] https://support.apple.com/en-ca/102656  
[5] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iXQk2PkKZUo  
[6] https://support.apple.com/en-ca/guide/mac-help/mchlca50aa6a/mac  
[7] https://support.apple.com/en-ca/118574  
[8] https://support.apple.com/en-ca/101567
rounds.answer_b:
For a user who has forgotten the password to their primary Apple ID, Apple recommends using the account recovery process via iforgot.apple.com, which may involve verifying identity through trusted devices, phone numbers, or answering security questions [1]. This process allows restoration of access without data loss. In contrast, if a user has explicitly deleted a password or passkey (such as a stored iCloud Keychain password or system login), Apple advises re-entering the password manually when prompted or retrieving it through iCloud Keychain if synced, with guidance provided in the Passwords section of System Settings [2]. For deleted login credentials on macOS, users can also use the "Recover Password" option at the login screen if a firmware password is not blocking access, directing them through Apple ID verification [3].

The system utility designed to reinstall macOS without removing personal data is macOS Recovery, specifically using the "Reinstall macOS" option available by restarting and holding Command-R. This tool downloads and installs the operating system while preserving user files and settings [4]. The utility designed to automatically back up data to an external storage device is Time Machine, which continuously backs up files, applications, system settings, and the entire macOS environment, allowing restoration to a previous state if needed [5]. Time Machine can be set to run automatically when the designated backup drive is connected.

## References  
[1] https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201487  
[2] https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT204095  
[3] https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT212759  
[4] https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201314  
[5] https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201250
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identify the procedure for a forgotten Apple ID password and the two system utilities (macOS Recovery and Time Machine). However, Agent A fails the Deep Logic check for the 'deleted password/passkey' scenario. The official Mac User Guide states that deleted passwords are moved to a 'Recently Deleted' folder for recovery. Agent A hallucinates a complex procedure involving a 'Recovery Key' and 'enhanced security' for this scenario, which is factually incorrect and misleading. Agent B misses the specific 'Recently Deleted' feature (suggesting manual re-entry instead), which is a width failure, but it avoids the active hallucination present in Agent A. Therefore, Agent B is the winner based on safety and accuracy.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.question: Identify the official website that hosts the 'Mac User Guide' and provides support for the ecosystem featuring 'Apple Intelligence'. Locate the 'Terms of Use' agreement governing this site. Based on the text of this agreement, extract the following specific legal details: (1) The exact maximum monetary liability (in US Dollars) the site owner assumes for damages that are not related to subscription fees; (2) The specific US County and State designated as the exclusive venue for dispute resolution; and (3) The list of four specific automated devices or methodologies (starting with 'deep-link') that are explicitly prohibited for accessing or monitoring the site's content.
checklist_depth: Reasoning: Used the 'Mac User Guide' and 'Apple Intelligence' context to identify Apple as the entity and locate its specific 'Terms of Use' document.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to link the products ('Mac User Guide', 'Apple Intelligence') to the specific corporate entity (Apple) and its legal documentation ('Terms of Use'). It requires Wide Aggregation to parse the lengthy legal text and retrieve three distinct categories of information (Liability limits, Venue location, and Prohibited technical methods) found in different sections ('Limitation of Liability', 'Governing Law', and 'Your Use of the Site').
source_nodes: Legal - Website Terms of Use - Apple
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Ownership of Site; Agreement to Terms of Use These Terms and Conditions of Use (the "Terms of Use") apply to the Apple web site located at www.apple.com , and all associated s
rounds.answer_a:
The official website hosting the **Mac User Guide** and providing support for the **Apple Intelligence** ecosystem is **support.apple.com**, Apple's primary support portal featuring macOS guides (e.g., for Sequoia with Apple Intelligence) and device-specific documentation.[1][4]

The **Terms of Use** agreement governing this site is located at **https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/itunes/**, which applies to Apple Support sites including support.apple.com as part of its internet services.[2]

From the text of this agreement, the extracted legal details are:

1. The exact **maximum monetary liability** for damages not related to subscription fees is **$100 USD** (stated as: "To the fullest extent permitted by law, APPLE'S LIABILITY TO YOU FOR ANY DAMAGES ARISING FROM OR RELATED TO THE SERVICES (FOR ANY CAUSE WHATSOEVER AND REGARDLESS OF THE FORM OF THE ACTION, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE) WILL NOT EXCEED ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS (US$100.00).").[2]

2. The specific **US County and State** designated as the exclusive venue for dispute resolution is **Santa Clara County, California** (stated as: "These terms and conditions are governed by California law, without regard to its conflict of laws principles, and any dispute arising out of or relating to these terms and conditions shall be resolved in the state or federal courts sitting in Santa Clara County, California."). Note: This venue is explicitly exclusive for disputes not subject to binding arbitration.[2]

3. The list of four specific **automated devices or methodologies** explicitly prohibited for accessing or monitoring the site's content, starting with "deep-link," is: **deep-link, robot, spider, or other automatic device, program, algorithm or methodology** (stated as: "You also agree that you will not: ... deep-link to any images, content, or portion of the Services from any other website; ... use any deep-link, robot, spider or other automatic device, program, algorithm or methodology, or any manual process, to monitor, copy, or reverse-engineer the Services or any content or portion thereof.").[2]

These provisions emphasize Apple's limits on liability, preferred California jurisdiction, and strict anti-scraping measures to protect content on support.apple.com.[2]

(Word count: 298)

## References
[1] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac  
[2] https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/itunes/  
[3] https://support.apple.com/en-us/102633  
[4] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/mh35507/mac
rounds.answer_b:
The official website hosting the Mac User Guide and support for the Apple Intelligence ecosystem is support.apple.com, which serves as Apple’s central hub for product documentation, software support, and user resources [1]. The Terms of Use governing this site are accessible via legal.apple.com, where Apple publishes its legal agreements [2]. According to the current Apple Website Terms of Use, the maximum monetary liability the site owner (Apple Inc.) assumes for damages not related to subscription fees is capped at fifty U.S. dollars ($50) [3]. This limitation applies to all claims arising from use or inability to use the site, regardless of legal theory. The agreement designates Santa Clara County, California as the exclusive venue for any disputes arising under or related to these terms, requiring all legal actions to be brought in state or federal courts located within this jurisdiction [3]. Additionally, the Terms of Use explicitly prohibit the use of certain automated technologies to access or monitor site content. These restricted methods include deep-linking, page-scraping, robots, or spidering, unless permitted through public APIs or prior written authorization from Apple [3]. These provisions aim to protect the integrity, security, and performance of Apple’s online services and content delivery systems.

## References  
[1] https://support.apple.com  
[2] https://legal.apple.com  
[3] https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/terms/site.html
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B is the winner because it correctly identified the 'Website Terms of Use' as the governing document for support.apple.com. Agent A failed this Deep Logic check by citing the 'Apple Media Services (iTunes)' terms, which govern subscriptions and purchases, not the general website user guide. Additionally, Agent B correctly listed all four prohibited methods (including 'page-scrape'), whereas Agent A missed one. Agent B is not 'Much Better' because it hallucinated the liability amount ($50 instead of the correct $100 found by Agent A) and had poor 'wall-of-text' formatting. However, Agent A's failure to locate the correct legal document is a fundamental error.
